data.title: WSPN's WUSD stablecoin is now on Coins.ph with PHP trading pair in the Philippines.
data.description: In Tortola, British Virgin Islands, on July 17, 2025, the Worldwide Stablecoin Payment Network (WSPN) revealed that its USD-backed stablecoin, WUSD, is now available for trading on Coins.ph, the prominent cryptocurrency exchange in the Philippines. This move marks a significant step for WSPN, as WUSD is now accessible to a wide user base in the Philippines through this partnership. Coins.ph, known for its active user community, offers a strong platform for the trading of WUSD, enhancing its liquidity and market presence in the region. This listing opens up new opportunities for users in the Philippines to engage with the stablecoin, promoting financial inclusion and providing access to a stable digital asset within the local crypto ecosystem.
The ASX 200 Index climbed 1% to a record high of 8,726.80 points.

CHINESE COMMERCE MINISTRY: TRADE-IN PROGRAMS DRIVE OVER 2.9 TRILLION YUAN IN SALES, WITH 400 MILLION PEOPLE BENEFITING FROM SUBSIDIES IN THE FIRST HALF OF THE YEAR. WILL INTRODUCE POLICIES TO FURTHER STIMULATE CONSUMPTION, BOOST SERVICES CONSUMPTION. CHINA HIT 14 FYP FDI
China's Commerce Minister emphasized that the country continues to exhibit robust trade resilience and momentum.
China's Commerce Minister highlighted that policy support for exports has defied trends amid a complex external environment.
China’s Commerce Minister affirmed that the country’s economic fundamentals remain solid in the long run.
China's Commerce Minister said the priority for the 15th Five-Year Plan is to build on the strengths of the 14th Plan and turn them into long-term mechanisms.
China's Commerce Minister said new policies will be introduced to further stimulate consumption and boost spending on services.
